# Repatriate Service: Full Site Index

> Specialist UK body repatriation service. In-depth country guides for the priority countries below, full country coverage worldwide, and country-to-country route guides. 24/7 availability. Full documentation coordination and embassy liaison.

---

## Country repatriation guides

Each country guide covers required documentation, local funeral director availability, embassy contact, airport information, common challenges, and a step-by-step process overview. Costs depend on the specifics of each case, so we give a case-specific estimate on contact rather than a fixed figure.

### P1 countries (with city-level guides)

**Spain**: /repatriation-from-spain/
Documentation: local death certificate, certified translation, embalming certificate, OFII transit permit. P1 cities: Madrid, Barcelona, Malaga, Ibiza, Tenerife.

**France**: /repatriation-from-france/
Documentation: acte de deces, laissez-passer mortuaire. P1 cities: Paris, Nice, Lyon.

**Thailand**: /repatriation-from-thailand/
Post-mortem often required. Documentation: death certificate, Foreign Affairs Division clearance, embalming certificate. P1 cities: Bangkok, Phuket, Koh Samui, Chiang Mai.

**USA**: /repatriation-from-usa/
State-level variation. P1 cities: New York, Los Angeles, Miami, Orlando.

**Greece**: /repatriation-from-greece/
Post-mortem common for accidents. P1 cities: Athens, Crete, Corfu, Rhodes.

**Turkey**: /repatriation-from-turkey/
Laissez-passer required. P1 cities: Istanbul, Antalya, Bodrum.

**Portugal**: /repatriation-from-portugal/
P1 cities: Lisbon, Algarve.

**Italy**: /repatriation-from-italy/
Regional variation. P1 cities: Rome, Milan, Venice.

**India**: /repatriation-from-india/
Documentation-intensive. P1 cities: Mumbai, Goa, Delhi, Chennai.

**Egypt**: /repatriation-from-egypt/
Bureaucratic delays common. P1 cities: Cairo, Hurghada, Sharm El Sheikh.

### P2 countries (country-level guides)

**Australia**: /repatriation-from-australia/
**Germany**: /repatriation-from-germany/
**Cyprus**: /repatriation-from-cyprus/
**UAE (Dubai)**: /repatriation-from-uae/
**Philippines**: /repatriation-from-philippines/
**South Africa**: /repatriation-from-south-africa/
**Kenya**: /repatriation-from-kenya/
**Canada**: /repatriation-from-canada/
**Brazil**: /repatriation-from-brazil/
**Mexico**: /repatriation-from-mexico/
**Morocco**: /repatriation-from-morocco/
**Dominican Republic**: /repatriation-from-dominican-republic/
**Sri Lanka**: /repatriation-from-sri-lanka/
**Indonesia**: /repatriation-from-indonesia/
**Cambodia**: /repatriation-from-cambodia/
**Vietnam**: /repatriation-from-vietnam/

---

## Country-to-country route guides

Beyond the UK-inbound country guides, the site publishes route guides for specific origin-to-destination corridors, for example Spain to the United Kingdom or UAE to Ireland. Each route guide covers the documentation, timeline, and step-by-step process for that corridor.

/routes/ : Full index of country-to-country repatriation corridors covered.

---

## First response guides

Step-by-step guides for the immediate hours after a death abroad. Each guide covers who to call first, what happens to the body, emergency contacts, documentation required, and timeline.

## Cremation abroad and ashes transport

For families choosing local cremation over full repatriation. Country guides covering cremation availability, documentation required, airline rules for carrying ashes, and how the options compare.

Note: cremation is not available in Muslim-majority countries (Turkey, Egypt, Morocco) under standard circumstances.

/cremation-transfer/ : Hub page

---

## Frequently asked questions

**How long does repatriation take?**
Typically 1 to 2 weeks for European countries with no complications. 2 to 4 weeks for Asia or where a post-mortem is required.

**Does travel insurance cover repatriation?**
Most comprehensive travel insurance policies include repatriation cover. The insurer appoints and funds the funeral director. Exclusions include pre-existing conditions not declared at purchase.

**Can I carry ashes on the plane?**
Yes, in most cases. Airlines allow ashes in carry-on baggage, subject to documentation. Call the airline before travel to confirm current policy.

**What is a zinc-lined coffin?**
A standard coffin with an airtight zinc inner lining, required by airline regulations for international transport of human remains.

**What does the British Embassy do?**
The Embassy can register the death, provide a list of local funeral directors, and offer consular assistance. They do not fund or coordinate repatriation.

**Is cremation abroad permitted for Muslims?**
No. Islamic law does not permit cremation. Full body repatriation is the only option for Muslim families.

**What is the role of the UK coroner?**
A UK coroner has jurisdiction over any repatriated body arriving in England, Wales or Northern Ireland. If unnatural or unexplained death is suspected, an inquest may be required. This is why cremation abroad is not suitable when a coroner may need to examine the body.
